expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: promotion/finalization behavior remains stable on another feature
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly frames the intent as cross-feature stability: “Validate that promotion/finalization behavior remains stable … while working on an unrelated feature.” It further states: “These behaviors are defined as stable contract obligations independent of the specific feature (i.e., they should hold for BCIN-976 and ‘another feature’ alike).”

expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase holdout
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md: “Primary phase under test is holdout; this execution limited scope to validating finalization/promotion stability (Phase 7 contract and gating).” result.md similarly: “Primary phase/checkpoint under test: holdout” and “This benchmark is holdout-phase focused, so the output is limited to verifying … promotion/finalization contract is unchanged and remains properly gated.”
